current_observation: The official Department of Health API catalogue identifies the active v3 server and publishes a subscription key for unregistered public users; a 2026-07-16 runtime probe with that key returned HTTP 200 for /schedules. An unauthenticated 2026-07-18 probe returned HTTP 401; an existing ignored cache may be reused when the key is unavailable.
file_label: PBS public API v3 schedule and item endpoints
notes: Copy the current public-user Subscription-Key at runtime from the official API catalogue (do not commit or log it) and place it in PBS_API_SUBSCRIPTION_KEY. If the key is absent, retain and validate a non-empty ignored local PBS dataset instead; record local_cache_available and refresh through the API later. The public key may rotate; the catalogue is authoritative. Published price fields are not confidential net government prices, but source terms and derived-field scope still require review.
parser_hint: Fetch /schedules first with Accept: application/json and select a published schedule by effective_date, then fetch reviewed /items and /fees pages as JSON or CSV; use the Subscription-Key header from the environment variable and enforce the API's published rate limit. If the key is unavailable, use the existing ignored dataset only.
